result.markdown:
--- title: "Build a design matrix" subtitle: "Model event timing with HRFs and inspect the generated regressors." jupyter: python3 --- ## Create Events ::: {#b7f3382b .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd import fmrimod as fm from fmrimod.design_colmap import design_colmap events = pd.DataFrame({ "onset": [0, 8, 16, 24, 32, 40], "condition": ["face", "scene", "face", "scene", "face", "scene"], "response_time": [0.8, 1.1, 0.7, 0.9, 1.0, 1.2], }) events ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html}
onset condition response_time
0 0 face 0.8
1 8 scene 1.1
2 16 face 0.7
3 24 scene 0.9
4 32 face 1.0
5 40 scene 1.2
``` ::: ::: ## Define the Acquisition Grid ::: {#7ba8d1d7 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} sf = fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=60, TR=1.0) sf.samples[:8] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` array([0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5, 6.5, 7.5]) ``` ::: ::: ## Build the Model The formula below creates condition-specific canonical HRF regressors. The result is an event model with a concrete design matrix and column metadata. ::: {#9cb98d6c .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} model = fm.event_model( 'onset ~ hrf(condition, basis="spmg1")', data=events, sampling_frame=sf, ) model.design_matrix.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` (60, 2) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#ffea257b .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} model.column_names ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ``` ['condition_condition.face', 'condition_condition.scene'] ``` ::: ::: ## Inspect Column Metadata ::: {#4577ee2b .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} design_colmap(model)[["col", "name", "condition", "role", "basis_name"]] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ```{=html}
col name condition role basis_name
0 1 condition_condition.face condition.face task SPMG1
1 2 condition_condition.scene condition.scene task SPMG1
``` ::: ::: ## Add a Parametric Modulator Continuous variables can enter the formula as event-level covariates. ::: {#4784872d .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} modulated = fm.event_model( 'onset ~ hrf(condition, basis="spmg1") + hrf(response_time, basis="spmg1")', data=events, sampling_frame=sf, ) modulated.design_matrix.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ``` (60, 3) ``` ::: :::
